evidence_posture.notes: Illustrative system synthesis for testing the differential reading template. It is not a Buchanan claim.

assemblage.name: Platform feed assemblage
assemblage.specificity_note: A platform interface that converts discontinuous image storage into a continuous, scrollable feed.

flows.name: images
flows.description: Photographs and visual posts appear as a continuous feed rather than as discontinuous files, albums, folders, or devices.

flows.name: attention
flows.description: User attention is drawn through scroll continuity, recommendation, novelty, and social comparison.

flows.name: affect
flows.description: Mood, comparison, desire, envy, recognition, and belonging circulate through the feed.

cuts.name: Removal of storage discontinuity
cuts.subtraction_or_interruption: Folder, device, album, and time boundaries are reduced or hidden.
cuts.effect: Images appear as a continuous stream.

cuts.name: Algorithmic selection threshold
cuts.subtraction_or_interruption: Only some images are surfaced while others disappear.
cuts.effect: The feed feels continuous while being constantly cut, ranked, and filtered.

captures.mechanism: Continuous feed design and algorithmic ranking extend time-on-platform.
Engagement produces behavioural traces that can be measured and monetised.
The feed organizes comparison, recognition, anxiety, desire, and social belonging.

desire: The assemblage produces desire for visibility, recognition, update, response, and belonging.
affect_intensity: The feed intensifies anticipation, comparison, distraction, pleasure, anxiety, and the compulsion to continue.
qualitative_difference: The same image differs when it is stored privately in a folder versus circulated publicly in a feed. The material image may be similar, but the assemblage and effect change.
line_of_flight: Possible exits include refusal of infinite scroll, return to private archives, slower curation, alternative platforms, or practices that break the capture of attention.
blocked_claims: Do not state that Buchanan directly made this social media argument unless later evidence is inserted.
Do not treat this as a citation-backed claim.
Do not infer psychological states of a specific user.
